name: HRC_COMMUNICATION_CONFIG
description: This table stores the configuration information for HCM Communications

columns.Name: CONFIG_ID
columns.Comments: Unique Identifier to identify the configuration record

columns.Name: PROCESS_ID
columns.Comments: Unique Identifier of the process to which the configuration belong to

columns.Name: TWO_WAY_COMM_SUPPORTED
columns.Comments: Parameter to indicate whether two way communication is supported or not

columns.Name: IS_BIP_CONTENT_SUPPORTED
columns.Comments: Indicate whether the BIP content is supported or not

columns.Name: BIP_TEMPLATE
columns.Comments: Details or the path of the BIP Template which is defined to show the report content.

columns.Name: IS_CUSTOM_ACTIONS_ALLOWED
columns.Comments: Indicates whether the custom actions are supported by the communication or not.

columns.Name: APPROVAL_CONFIGURATION_JSON
columns.Comments: Json Configuration record, which will indicate the different levels of approval and rules on each level.

columns.Name: IS_SMS_SUPPORTED
columns.Comments: indicates whether sms mode is supported by the communication or not

columns.Name: IS_EMAIL_SUPPORTED
columns.Comments: indicates whether email mode is supported by the communication or not

columns.Name: IS_PUSH_SUPPORTED
columns.Comments: indicates whether push mode is supported by the communication or not

columns.Name: DELEGATION_CATEGORY
columns.Comments: value of the category corresponding to which this process will be mapped for approval delegations

columns.Name: ENTERPRISE_ID
columns.Comments: Unique Identifier of the enterprise to filter out the records which does not belong to.

columns.Name: MODULE_ID
columns.Comments: Seed Data Framework: indicates the module that owns the row. A module is an entry in Application Taxonomy such as a Logical Business Area. When the MODULE_ID column exists and the owner of the row is not specified, then the Seed Data Framework will not extract the row as seed data.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: SEED_DATA_SOURCE
columns.Comments: Source of seed data record. A value of 'BULK_SEED_DATA_SCRIPT' indicates that record was bulk loaded. Otherwise, specifies the name of the seed data file.

columns.Name: PURGE_PERIOD
columns.Comments: Indicates purge period for a communication process

columns.Name: SHOW_IN_CONFIG_UI
columns.Comments: Indicates whether to show the configuration entry in message configuration ui or not.

columns.Name: DISABLE_COMMUNICATION
columns.Comments: Indicates whether to enable the communication for the process or not. If this is false it will result into error.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET1
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET1. Context values are Y or N.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET2
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET2. Context values are Y or N.
